channel.title: Home on CTF Writeups
channel.description: Recent content in Home on CTF Writeups

item.title: bashtille
item.description:
What was supposed to be a binary exploit challenge quickly turned into a full exploration that revealed unintended access to CTF challenges and their flags.

This write-up is not focused on the challenge itself, but on the process that led to discovering this unexpected access.

Description

Through gloomy vaults where the light of day had never shown, past hideous doors of dark dens and cages, down cavernous flights of steps, and again up steep rugged ascents of stone and brick, more like dry waterfalls than staircases…

Description

Ok hotshot, this is it, the only thing standing between you and slightly less internet anonymity.

7-zip password: flare

item.title: ropvm
item.description:
Description

This isn’t your usual machine.

Think differently and precisely. Then the flag is yours.

nc chal.h4c.cddc2025.xyz 31123

Download chal.zip

item.title: Decrypting Firmware
item.description:
Description

Bootloader Base Address : 0x21000000

Kernel Base Address : 0xc0008000

Download chal.tar

item.title: pwnymalloc
item.description:
Description

Author: Akhil

i’m tired of hearing all your complaints. pwnymalloc never complains.

ncat --ssl pwnymalloc.chal.uiuc.tf 1337

Download alloc.c

item.title: HeapBaby
item.description:
Description

I honestly didn’t copy the description and they always don’t open the challenges after the CTF, so that’s that.

I solved this challenge after the CTF ended, so it isn’t tested on the remote server.

item.title: widget
item.description:
Description

I seem to have lost my gadgets.

nc challs.actf.co 31320

Download Dockerfile

item.title: Broken RSA
item.description:
Description

My friend had a secret message for me. However, he made a mistake when encrypting the message, and now I am unable to decrypt it! Could you decrypt and recover the message for me?

item.title: ☁️ Cloudy with a chance of meatballs
item.description:
Description

Today in school, I learnt to code in HTML! View my brand new website! www.lncctf2023.tk

item.title: User Application Firewall
item.description:
Description

Created by MetaCTF

Our penetration testing team is just one server away from getting access to all of C3’s networks! This one’s proving to be very tough, as the only thing that’s running is their User Application Firewall. The company claims this service is hardened against any buffer overflow and other stack-based vulnerabilities. They even give you the compiled binary , their source code and libc that they used to run this service! Can you break in?

item.title: 🔴 Got Any Games?
item.description:
Category Solves
Hard 33

Description

Author: @M_alpha#3534

I created this new guessing game for Android. To deter cheaters, I made sure to include advanced military grade encryption.
Please don’t cheat, Thanks!

Download the files below.

Special thank you to INTIGRITI for sponsoring NahamCon EU CTF 2022! This challenge is dedicated to them as a token of gratitude.

item.title: Asmuth Shares
item.description:
Description

Asmuth wants to share something with you!

  • chal.py
  • out.txt

TL;DR

  • Use Chinese Remainder Theorem on all shares.
  • Use result from CRT and perform mod m0 to get long value of FLAG.
  • Stonks

Solution

Analysis

PLAINTEXT 
Public (m0): 0x61f42f24878101c5186ec677cd44a2b14071b9a69260a026fb385482123f2bd647f74e8c569d3cad1c2d48ad6da58ceddd021c5d

Share 0 (s0, m1): (0x60ec3bb546f5905293052cc6154d632942ef447f3983665f34651637c34c384970abf40095368ffd426f5139ec3d1e07a868a830, 0x6307ff90da6fa375ccf1803b158219da8b9e44fb6859c2f18ce7095584136dfd805a5ac4ecea326c10863ed6e2e39ac82abc9c49)
Share 1 (s1, m2): (0x58675225153f997cd4f4d5bcc0ec9febf60ca7e7aa1759ae8a7037ad0dc4ce45ec79e49c3a1f8bb011adadc57e7d8a39bb845cad, 0x6cdc758a8d408a4ef39acd33f9677c268d685c9ddb3276fb970ad6fe2ebe549daace27fd7b05ca495ba3fe7fef9f9bbd23baf44d)
Share 2 (s2, m3): (0x3d045f39ea1b2f9a5486b16fef3b9a99fd68de9d2d9235c14b6254d3dedb40f7bc8f2087de7a111671426d83b6c9f1709ab90d89, 0x727113b40e9c974febb4e7cb2952083f96a97d1b17093963ee7c474fe780f5bf31241b540470c949593b363ca2c84d73584eb2f3)
Share 3 (s3, m4): (0x3e27c86088d7c7725814c9efe17909c408a1ddca3f0397c81d142b03d6329ef30941b558f22dc8bc20e752e4a6088a7067e76565, 0x7739b291c0ba89eec860dc8a6b018acb5d05d879504285a079b2785edac2cd543c6a86589071835d74f63524952910944b0977e7)
Public (m0): 0x61f42f24878101c5186ec677cd44a2b14071b9a69260a026fb385482123f2bd647f74e8c569d3cad1c2d48ad6da58ceddd021c5d Share 0 (s0, m1): (0x60ec3bb546f5905293052cc6154d632942ef447f3983665f34651637c34c384970abf40095368ffd426f5139ec3d1e07a868a830, 0x6307ff90da6fa375ccf1803b158219da8b9e44fb6859c2f18ce7095584136dfd805a5ac4ecea326c10863ed6e2e39ac82abc9c49) Share 1 (s1, m2): (0x58675225153f997cd4f4d5bcc0ec9febf60ca7e7aa1759ae8a7037ad0dc4ce45ec79e49c3a1f8bb011adadc57e7d8a39bb845cad, 0x6cdc758a8d408a4ef39acd33f9677c268d685c9ddb3276fb970ad6fe2ebe549daace27fd7b05ca495ba3fe7fef9f9bbd23baf44d) Share 2 (s2, m3): (0x3d045f39ea1b2f9a5486b16fef3b9a99fd68de9d2d9235c14b6254d3dedb40f7bc8f2087de7a111671426d83b6c9f1709ab90d89, 0x727113b40e9c974febb4e7cb2952083f96a97d1b17093963ee7c474fe780f5bf31241b540470c949593b363ca2c84d73584eb2f3) Share 3 (s3, m4): (0x3e27c86088d7c7725814c9efe17909c408a1ddca3f0397c81d142b03d6329ef30941b558f22dc8bc20e752e4a6088a7067e76565, 0x7739b291c0ba89eec860dc8a6b018acb5d05d879504285a079b2785edac2cd543c6a86589071835d74f63524952910944b0977e7)
PYTHON 
from Crypto.Util.number import getPrime, getRandomRange, bytes_to_long

FLAG = bytes_to_long(open("flag.txt", "rb").read())
N = FLAG.bit_length() + 2**7

m = sorted([ getPrime(N) for _ in range(5) ])
share = []

while ((m[1]*m[2]*m[3] < m[0]*m[3]*m[4]) and m[4]<m[3]):
	m[4] = getPrime(N)

M = m[1]*m[2]
alpha = getRandomRange(2, M)
k = 4

for i in range(k):
 s = (FLAG + alpha*m[0]) % m[i+1]
 share.append(s)

with open("out.txt", 'w') as f:
 f.write(f"Public (m0): {hex(m[0])}\n\n")

 for i in range(len(share)):
 f.write(f"Share {i} (s{i}, m{i+1}): ({hex(share[i])}, {hex(m[i+1])})\n")
from Crypto.Util.number import getPrime, getRandomRange, bytes_to_long FLAG = bytes_to_long(open("flag.txt", "rb").read()) N = FLAG.bit_length() + 2**7 m = sorted([ getPrime(N) for _ in range(5) ]) share = [] while ((m[1]*m[2]*m[3] < m[0]*m[3]*m[4]) and m[4]<m[3]): m[4] = getPrime(N) M = m[1]*m[2] alpha = getRandomRange(2, M) k = 4 for i in range(k): s = (FLAG + alpha*m[0]) % m[i+1] share.append(s) with open("out.txt", 'w') as f: f.write(f"Public (m0): {hex(m[0])}\n\n") for i in range(len(share)): f.write(f"Share {i} (s{i}, m{i+1}): ({hex(share[i])}, {hex(m[i+1])})\n")

We are given the values of quite a few variables in the out.txt file.

item.title: OMG RNG!
item.description:
Description

Gifts for everyone!

nc lab-1.ctf.standcon.n0h4ts.com 7903

item.title: Lost In the Deep
item.description:
Description

You’ve been swimming, and encounter a submerged mangrove forest. You enter it to explore, and without knowing it, you’re lost. How do you escape?

item.title: New Math!
item.description:
Description

Hooray for new math! New whoo hoo math!

Wrap the flag in STANDCON22{}

item.title: Wait For The Day
item.description:
Description

Momma bought a Christmas gift for you. She says it’s something you love! But Christmas is like more than 6 months away :/ Try to peek in and find out what the gift is, before the Christmas eve ;)

item.title: Want a byte?
item.description:
Description

Scrolling through my projects, I found some old project code. I remember this code had some “backdoors”. But since I mistakenly deleted utils.py, now I cannot understand my own code! Can you help me make sense out of it? You will be rewarded with a flag for your help.

item.title: Blendin's Plain Plane
item.description:
DifficultyPoints
Easy150

Description

The challenge lies ahead, in Blendin’s plain plane, do you have what it takes to find the flag’s reign?

item.title: Deobf Me
item.description:
Difficulty Points
Medium 300

Description

The symbols are speaking, what do they want?

[Download chall (1).py](/gitbook/assets/chall (1).py)

item.title: Chilling Beat
item.description:
DifficultyPoints
Easy150

Description

This looks like a fun file filled with cool beats. Enjoy the song while you can!

Download ChillingBeat.osz

item.title: Chilling Beat 2
item.description:
Difficulty Points
Easy 150

Description

I am now given these lines of data… what does these have to do with any chilling beats???

item.title: Flag Shop
item.description:
Difficulty Points
Easy 150

Description

I’m only selling 1 product and that’s flags! Please make sure you calculate how much money you have before coming here.

item.title: ECB Xoracle
item.description:
Difficulty Points
Easy 150

Description

I implemented an additional step in my encryption/decryption service and declare its name as “Xoracle”. It should be more secure, right?

item.title: Mathematics of Cryptography
item.description:
Difficulty Points
Easy 150

Description

The Defenders heard that there are some Math legends among us and have reached out for assistance in breaking one of the Uprisers’ security defenses.

item.title: Private Assertion Nexus
item.description:
Difficulty Points
Easy 150

Description

I’ve heard talk about a simple method of communication, but I’m not sure what it is. Luckily I found some traffic of the said communication, but I’m too noob to figure it out. Please help me crack this enigma of a transmission.
